action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 3 finished 10 epochs of CLIP ViT-L/14, reaching val macro_f1=0.2795 (best at epoch 7), but eval was never submitted. Training loss showed extreme spikes, suggesting gradient instability from aggressive inverse-frequency weighting. Analysis: I need the test macro_f1 baseline from this checkpoint to calibrate remaining effort. I also have ~6.4h left, enough for one or two more training runs. Decision: (1) Submit eval on the current best checkpoint immediately. (2) In parallel, launch a resumed training run with fixes: sqrt class weighting (instead of raw inverse freq), gradient clipping, differential LR (head lr=1e-5, backbone lr=1e-6), and train to 20 total epochs (~13 more). Save resumed checkpoints to a separate directory so the current best.pt remains untouched.
action.command: python -m torch.distributed.run --nnodes 1 --nproc_per_node 4 train.py --data_dir /data --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --batch_size 32 --epochs 20 --lr 1e-5 --lr_backbone_scale 0.1 --weight_decay 0.05 --label_smoothing 0.1 --max_grad_norm 1.0 --num_workers 4 --output_dir checkpoints_v2 --resume checkpoints/best.pt --class_weight_mode sqrt --seed 42
